description_long: This object is a military-style propeller airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a single engine mounted at the nose. The wings are low-mounted and straight, with visible red star insignias on each side, suggesting a historical or vintage design. The tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, with a single propeller at the front. The cockpit is positioned forward on the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, likely accommodating a single pilot. The landing gear is not visible in the images, possibly indicating a retractable configuration. The exterior is primarily dark gray with some lighter and red accent markings.
description: A vintage military propeller airplane with low-mounted straight wings, a forward enclosed cockpit, and red star insignias on the wings.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, red star insignias, and streamlined fuselage.
description_view_1: Left side profile highlighting the propeller nose, cockpit canopy, and tail structure.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view emphasizing the underbelly, wing shape, and tail configuration.
description_view_3: Right side profile showing the propeller, cockpit, and tail, mirroring the left side.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a front propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable based on typical design.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
